novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The related-work comparison misses Kallus and Zhou 2018/2021 on confounding-robust policy improvement under nominal propensity-weight uncertainty, a closer policy-learning prior than generic DROPE; it appears non-subsuming but must be named.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Kallus and Zhou 2018 NeurIPS / 2021 Management Science as a direct comparator, explaining that their nominal propensity-weight uncertainty and minimax policy-improvement objective do not impose the same paired latent-response and sup-norm reward-regression compatibility tube.
Specialize one corollary to KL or odds-ratio sensitivity and binary actions, so the gap to Hess et al. and Ishikawa-He is algebraically visible rather than only described in prose.
For Conjecture 2, include a sketched two-action numerical witness template with non-endpoint rewards and strict inequalities; this would preempt witness-triviality concerns at Stage 0.

recommended_next_step: Patch §5 and the Conjecture 1/2 novelty blocks with the missing Kallus-Zhou comparison, then keep the kernel unchanged.
